name: Aripicker.github.io
body:
# Hey Buddy  


				

My name is Ari Picker. I recently graduated from a 3 month, front end development, intensive course at [The Iron Yard](http://theironyard.com/). I used to do [this](http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=JY4sR_pTGWU#t=00m19s), and now I do [this](http://paulsinternetsecurityblog.files.wordpress.com/2011/03/screaming-at-laptop.jpg).

I'm artsy-fartsy, a father of 2 and I can hold my own on or off the mic. After recently having my second child, it became glaringly apparent that I needed a skill to pay the bills. Coding has proven to be challenging, rewarding and fun. My goal is continued improvement through yelling at my computer. Right now I'm a pretty competent spell caster, but that's not enough, I want to be a great white hat wizard.



###Below you will find a few projects I have been working on-


##### [irnyrd-proj](http://pickra.github.io/irnyrd-proj/) 
This is a tiny responsive piece I made for The Iron Yard. [Here](https://github.com/Pickra/irnyrd-proj) is the corresponding github repo. It's in [production](http://theironyard.com/education/academy/front-end-engineering/graduates/) on their site.

####  Still in development -

##### [Politi-Troll](http://pickra.github.io/Political-Troll/)
This app easily looks up US Senator's and Congressman's voting records. It shows what they voted on, how they voted and when the vote happened. I built it in Backbone and used JSONP to retrieve data from the [govtrack api](https://www.govtrack.us/developers/api). [Here](https://github.com/Pickra/Political-Troll) is the corresponding github repo.

##### [to-do](http://pickra.github.io/to-do/)
A todo app using [Parse](https://www.parse.com/) as the backend. [Here](https://github.com/Pickra/to-do) is the corresponding github repo.

#####  [make-plugin](http://pickra.github.io/make-plugin/demo/#) 
A fun little plugin that smacks the user. I envisioned this being used for validation. I obviously could have chosen a better name. [Here](https://github.com/Pickra/make-plugin) is the corresponding github repo.

##### [mobber](http://pickra.github.io/mobber/)
This was an assignment I was given while at The Iron Yard to replicate a page based off of a .png; and nothing else. [Here](https://github.com/Pickra/mobber) is the corresponding github repo.

## Thanks for checking out my work. More to come.

![](me-winking.gif)
